content.text: Ah, I see the test is inherited from the `UnivariateStatisticAbstractTest`. Let me read that file:

content.content: Read lines 415-444 of 621 from src/main/java/org/apache/commons/math/stat/descriptive/moment/Variance.java

content.text: Let me look at the weighted variance method with segment:

content.content: Read lines 461-495 of 621 from src/main/java/org/apache/commons/math/stat/descriptive/moment/Variance.java

content.text: Let me find the actual implementation part of that method:

content.content: Read lines 491-525 of 621 from src/main/java/org/apache/commons/math/stat/descriptive/moment/Variance.java

content.text:
Perfect! I found the bug. On line 509-511, the code sums the weights across the entire `weights.length` instead of only from `begin` to `begin + length`. This is the bug that causes the test to fail. It should sum only the weights in the specified segment.

Let me complete the reading:
